titulo: Triples with Bitwise AND Equal To Zero
enunciado:
Given an integer array nums, return 
the number of 
AND triples
.


An 
AND triple
 is a triple of indices 
(i, j, k)
 such that:




0 <= i < nums.length


0 <= j < nums.length


0 <= k < nums.length


nums[i] & nums[j] & nums[k] == 0
, where 
&
 represents the bitwise-AND operator.




 


Example 1:




Input:
 nums = [2,1,3]

Output:
 12

Explanation:
 We could choose the following i, j, k triples:
(i=0, j=0, k=1) : 2 & 2 & 1
(i=0, j=1, k=0) : 2 & 1 & 2
(i=0, j=1, k=1) : 2 & 1 & 1
(i=0, j=1, k=2) : 2 & 1 & 3
(i=0, j=2, k=1) : 2 & 3 & 1
(i=1, j=0, k=0) : 1 & 2 & 2
(i=1, j=0, k=1) : 1 & 2 & 1
(i=1, j=0, k=2) : 1 & 2 & 3
(i=1, j=1, k=0) : 1 & 1 & 2
(i=1, j=2, k=0) : 1 & 3 & 2
(i=2, j=0, k=1) : 3 & 2 & 1
(i=2, j=1, k=0) : 3 & 1 & 2



Example 2:




Input:
 nums = [0,0,0]

Output:
 27



 


Constraints:




1 <= nums.length <= 1000


0 <= nums[i] < 2
16
